heading: Definitions
label: 3902 Definitions
notes: References in TextThe Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, referred to in par. (9), is not set out in the Code. See Publication of Harmonized Tariff Schedule note set out under .section 1202 of this title

Change of NameCommittee on International Relations of House of Representatives changed to Committee on Foreign Affairs of House of Representatives by House Resolution No. 6, One Hundred Tenth Congress, .Jan. 5, 2007
Effective DateFor effective date of this section, see , set out as a note under .section 15 of Pub. L. 108–19section 3901 of this title

children.heading: Appropriate congressional committees
children.label: 1 Appropriate congressional committees
children.text: The term “appropriate congressional committees” means the Committee on Ways and Means and the Committee on International Relations of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Finance and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate.

children.heading: Controlled through the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme
children.label: 2 Controlled through the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme
A
children.text: carried out in accordance with the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme, as set forth in regulations promulgated by the President; or

children.label: B
children.text: controlled under a system determined by the President to meet substantially the standards, practices, and procedures of the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme.

children.heading: Exporting authority
children.label: 3 Exporting authority
children.text: The term “exporting authority” means 1 or more entities designated by a Participant from whose territory a shipment of rough diamonds is being exported as having the authority to validate the Kimberley Process Certificate.

children.heading: Importing authority
children.label: 4 Importing authority
children.text: The term “importing authority” means 1 or more entities designated by a Participant into whose territory a shipment of rough diamonds is imported as having the authority to enforce the laws and regulations of the Participant regulating imports, including the verification of the Kimberley Process Certificate accompanying the shipment.

children.heading: Kimberley Process Certificate
children.label: 5 Kimberley Process Certificate
children.text: The term “Kimberley Process Certificate” means a forgery resistant document of a Participant that demonstrates that an importation or exportation of rough diamonds has been controlled through the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme and contains the minimum elements set forth in Annex I to the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme.

children.heading: Kimberley Process Certification Scheme
children.label: 6 Kimberley Process Certification Scheme
children.text: The term “Kimberley Process Certification Scheme” means those standards, practices, and procedures of the international certification scheme for rough diamonds presented in the document entitled “Kimberley Process Certification Scheme” referred to in the Interlaken Declaration on the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme for Rough Diamonds of .November 5, 2002

children.heading: Participant
children.label: 7 Participant
children.text: The term “Participant” means a state, customs territory, or regional economic integration organization identified by the Secretary of State.

children.heading: Person
children.label: 8 Person
children.text: The term “person” means an individual or entity.

children.heading: Rough diamond
children.label: 9 Rough diamond
children.text: The term “rough diamond” means any diamond that is unworked or simply sawn, cleaved, or bruted and classifiable under subheading 7102.10, 7102.21, or 7102.31 of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 10 United States
children.text: The term “United States”, when used in the geographic sense, means the several States, the District of Columbia, and any commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: any United States citizen or any alien admitted for permanent residence into the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: any entity organized under the laws of the United States or any jurisdiction within the United States (including its foreign branches); and

children.label: C
children.text: any person in the United States.
